name: Babylon Health
tagline: AI-powered virtual primary care and symptom checker
description: Once valued at $4.2B after a SPAC IPO, Babylon Health's AI diagnostics claims were disputed by The Lancet. It went bankrupt in 2023 and was sold for parts.
story: Babylon Health was founded in London in 2013 by Ali Parsa with the mission of putting an accessible healthcare AI in every pocket. The company built an AI symptom checker and virtual care platform, going public via SPAC in 2021 at a $4.2 billion valuation. But the cracks were deep. A Lancet study disputed Babylon's claims that its AI could diagnose as well as human doctors. Safety concerns were raised with UK regulators. The company posted a $274 million net loss in 2022 alone on approximately $1 billion in revenue — the math never worked. US operations filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy in August 2023, followed by UK administration in September. Babylon was sold for parts, its AI ambitions reduced to a cautionary tale about overpromising in healthcare.
review_flags: Funding of $1.2B may include SPAC/public market capital. Wikipedia lists only ~$635M in disclosed private funding rounds (Series A-C). The $1.2B figure likely includes capital raised via the 2021 SPAC merger. Consider clarifying whether this is private funding only or total capital raised including public markets.
